channel.title: EMERGENZ Biosecurity Intelligence Dashboard
channel.description: High-severity biosecurity signals and recent authority/media coverage. Generated 2026-05-27T10:08:29.515Z.

item.title: [ACTION] Ebola disease — Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo
item.description: WHO has declared the Bundibugyo ebolavirus outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, with Africa CDC confirming active outbreak monitoring in both the DRC and Uganda. Multi-health-zone spread in eastern DRC continues; cross-border movement elevates geographic risk. Specific case and fatality counts require direct verification against the current WHO Disease Outbreak News (who.int/emergencies/disease-outbreak-news) or Africa CDC epidemic intelligence report.

item.title: [CONCERN] Andes virus hantavirus cluster — MV Hondius travel-linked
item.description: Andes hantavirus cluster linked to a South America–Antarctica expedition cruise aboard MV Hondius. 11 reported cases (9 confirmed, 2 probable) and 3 deaths across multiple countries, with multinational case-finding and post-exposure monitoring continuing.

item.title: [CONCERN] Measles — United States
item.description: CDC confirmed 1,952 measles cases across 40 jurisdictions in 2026 (as of May 21), representing 29 outbreaks with 93% of cases outbreak-associated. No deaths reported in 2026. U.S. kindergartener MMR coverage has declined to 92.5% (from 95.2% in 2019–2020), leaving approximately 286,000 kindergarteners unvaccinated — the structural driver of sustained outbreak activity.

item.title: [CONCERN] Mpox — clade I, Africa & travel-linked importations
item.description: Mpox clade I and travel-linked importation monitoring remain operationally significant for clinical recognition, specimen routing, and laboratory clade differentiation. Verify current country-level trends and any importation details against specific Africa CDC, WHO, or CDC updates before publication.

item.title: [CONCERN] Cholera — sub-Saharan Africa, including Republic of Congo
item.description: Africa CDC reported 40,707 cholera cases continent-wide from January 1 through May 14, 2026 — a 65% year-over-year decline compared to the same period in 2025. Despite the overall volume decrease, case fatality ratios remain elevated in conflict-affected and low-healthcare-access settings. Sudan, Ethiopia, Somalia, Mozambique, Malawi, and Zimbabwe continue reporting active transmission.

item.title: [CONCERN] Chikungunya — global outbreak activity
item.description: Chikungunya surveillance remains active across parts of the Americas and other mosquito-active regions. Verify current country burden and case totals against specific PAHO or WHO updates before publication.

item.title: Google News: Measles: Death toll rises to 560 with five more deaths in 24 hours - The Daily Star
item.description: Measles: Death toll rises to 560 with five more deaths in 24 hours The Daily Star

item.description: Measles death toll climbs to 560 in Bangladesh, five more die in 24 hours newagebd.net

item.title: UKHSA: Official Statistics: Weekly all-cause mortality surveillance: week ending 21 June 2026
item.description: How the actual number of deaths in England compare with expected numbers for each week.

item.title: UKHSA: Official Statistics: Cover of vaccination evaluated rapidly (COVER) programme 2025 to 2026: quarterly data
item.description: Percentage of children immunised under the cover of vaccination evaluated rapidly (COVER) programme for quarter 4, from 1 January 2026 to 31 March 2026.

item.title: UKHSA: Official Statistics: Respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) older adults vaccination coverage in England: April 2026
item.description: Insights on the vaccine uptake and commentary on the RSV (respiratory syncytial virus) vaccination programme for older adults.

item.title: UKHSA: Official Statistics: Respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) maternal vaccination coverage in England: January 2026
item.description: Insights on the vaccine uptake and commentary on the RSV (respiratory syncytial virus) vaccination programme for pregnant women.

item.description: Dozens of hikers fall ill along Pacific Crest Trail near Wrightwood Victorville Daily Press

item.description: Hantavirus cases from cruise ship outbreak rise to 13 Türkiye Today

item.title: Google News: Contagion of conspiracy theories makes Ebola epidemic harder to contain - The Washington Post
item.description: Contagion of conspiracy theories makes Ebola epidemic harder to contain The Washington Post

item.title: Google News: Thailand to Quarantine Travelers From Ebola Areas for 21 Days - Bloomberg.com
item.description: Thailand to Quarantine Travelers From Ebola Areas for 21 Days Bloomberg.com

item.title: Google News: Hantavirus cases from cruise outbreak rise to 13 following new case in Spain: WHO - TRT World
item.description: Hantavirus cases from cruise outbreak rise to 13 following new case in Spain: WHO TRT World

item.title: BBC Health: Endometriosis: 'I thought I was dying'
item.description: The BBC's Ellie Colton shares her experience of living with endometriosis - and meets a scientist hoping to cut the length of time it takes to be diagnosed with a simple new test.

item.title: Google News: Public health hot topic: Hantavirus - Islands' Sounder
item.description: Public health hot topic: Hantavirus Islands' Sounder

item.title: Google News: Should you avoid cruises after recent hantavirus concerns? Experts answer - The Indian Express
item.description: Should you avoid cruises after recent hantavirus concerns? Experts answer The Indian Express

item.title: UKHSA: Research: Hantavirus statistical releases
item.description: Statistical release in response to cases of hantavirus in the UK connected to the outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship.

item.title: STAT News: Opinion: Sen. Dick Durbin: Trump is letting Big Tobacco target children
item.description: “Trump has willingly exposed an entire generation of children to Big Tobacco’s nicotine addiction,” writes Sen. Dick Durbin.

item.title: STAT News: Opinion: The patients demanding unvaccinated blood transfusions
item.description: If you've been vaccinated against Covid-19, some patients might not want your donated blood.

item.title: STAT News: STAT+: How Stanford patients help expose ‘fault lines’ in health AI adoption
item.description: Stanford Health Care started asking patients about new AI tools before they are implemented. Here's what patients are telling them.

item.title: STAT News: ‘Are we just going to give up and die like every other generation?’
item.description: Inside the longevity conference that brings together founders, investors, biohackers, and the generally death-averse to discuss how to forestall — or even beat — our demise.

item.title: STAT News: Opinion: It’s the end of science as we know it, and I feel fine
item.description: “Science itself is inherently resilient — that is, after all, why it’s science,” writes Jonathan Jackson.
